findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Merged table cells for rows 11/14/2013 and 9/11/2014 were split, duplicating content across columns
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the 11/14/2013 and 9/11/2014 rows have cells that span across both the 'Jordan Lake Water Supply Allocations' and 'Cape Fear River Basin Water Supply Plan' columns as a single merged cell. In the HTML, these merged cells are split into two separate columns with the full text duplicated in each (e.g., 'Final Water Supply Plan and DWR's Allocation Recommendations presented at the EMC's November 2013 meeting.' appears identically in both columns).

findings.description: Merged cell spanning Jordan Lake and Cape Fear columns for 11/14/2013 and 9/11/2014 rows is split into duplicate cells, repeating the same text in both columns.
findings.evidence: PDF 11/14/2013 row: 'Final Water Supply Plan and DWR's Allocation Recommendations presented at the EMC's November 2013 meeting.' spans across Jordan Lake and Cape Fear columns as one merged cell. HTML duplicates this text into two separate <td> cells. Same issue with 9/11/2014 row: 'EMC decision at the September 2014 meeting.' duplicated into both columns.

findings.description: 'EMC Model Approval' appears in a single merged cell in the PDF's Hydrologic Model column but is duplicated across four rows (1/1/2013, 2/1/2013, 4/1/2013, 5/1/2013) in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'EMC Model Approval' once in a vertically merged cell in the Cape Fear – Neuse River Basin Hydrologic Model column spanning approximately from 2/1/2013 onward. HTML repeats it in four separate row cells.
